data.narrative:
LeoLabs is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company founded by Dan Ceperley that has raised $94M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Cowboy Space Corporation at $2.0B, Aalyria at $1.3B, and Hubble Network at $400M) and 5 public space and aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B progression, yielding $179M. Peer-median analysis across the three private comparables produces $1.3B, reflecting the wide dispersion in the comp set ($400M to $2.0B). Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied as LeoLabs' revenue is not disclosed. The two applicable methods diverge substantially, with stage-progression anchored to capital raised and peer-median reflecting market pricing of funded competitors in the same stage.

Triangulation of these methods supports a valuation range of $152M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $739M. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around revenue scale, unit economics, and whether peer multiples reflect sustainable valuations or sector exuberance. The peer-median method carries greater weight given the direct comparability of stage and sector, though the extreme spread within the three-company peer set limits confidence in any single point estimate. Additional clarity on revenue trajectory and customer concentration would materially sharpen this range.
